objective: Bind the complete accepted feature-lock fingerprint and its closed selected module/effective-marker/dependency inventory into opaque module-nominal Rust activation capabilities and one distinct consumer-issued activation receipt, while keeping absence inert and existing runtime facades unchanged.
non_scope: String, Int64, chunks, multi-channel, broader discovery, adapters, transport-loop refactoring, or changes to existing runtime constructor signatures.
Devices, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, ambient activation, official implementation source, private paths/evidence, crate splitting, or Manifold authority.
outputs: One dependency-free accepted-lock contract that rejects stale fingerprints, unknown or duplicate module selections, marker mismatch, and incomplete declared dependency closure.
Opaque module-nominal activation capabilities and a distinct consumer-issued receipt binding the exact lock fingerprint/revision, consumer, selected modules, effective markers, and accepted outcome.
Valid and damaged Rust/JSON fixtures plus focused activation, owner, public-boundary, and workflow checks.

acceptance.proof: The accepted exact lock fingerprint/revision and complete closed selection are required; stale, unknown, duplicate, missing dependency, and marker-damaged inputs reject typed.
Only admitted module-nominal capabilities can be obtained, and a separate consumer receipt retains the exact accepted lock and ordered selection/marker outcome.
Unselected modules yield no capability, current runtime facades remain unchanged, and no runtime effect or dependency is added.
Public-safe source-only activation evidence adds no devices, private paths, ambient effects, official-source intake, or Manifold authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the lock-bound activation capability and exact nonclaims.
Describe closed lock admission and preserved runtime facades.
Record artifact roles, opaque capabilities, receipt ownership, and dependency-closure boundary.
Route valid/damaged activation fixtures and checks.
Review closed activation and lane boundary; current guidance already covers the unit.
Review fingerprinted capability and receipt separation; current guidance already covers the unit.

commit_policy: Commit proposal, reviewed lifecycle transitions, implementation/fixtures/instructions, validation evidence, acceptance, and push preparation intentionally; preserve every failed attempt.
